text-stone-400","children":[["$","$L7",null,{"href":"/authors/sara-osei","className":"flex items-center gap-2 group","children":[["$","img",null,{"src":"https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1438761681033-6461ffad8d80?w=400&h=400&fit=crop&auto=format&q=80","alt":"Sara Osei","className":"w-8 h-8 rounded-full object-cover object-top"}],["$","div",null,{"children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-stone-700 dark:text-stone-200 text-sm group-hover:text-orange-600 transition-colors","children":"Sara Osei"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xs text-stone-400","children":"Productivity Researcher"}]]}]]}],["$","span",null,{"className":"flex items-center gap-1","children":[["$","$L6",null,{"ref":"$undefined","iconNode":[["circle",{"cx":"12","cy":"12","r":"10","key":"1mglay"}],["path",{"d":"M12 6v6l4 2","key":"mmk7yg"}]],"className":"lucide-clock","size":13}]," ","11 min read"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"March 21, 2026"}]]}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-10 pb-16","children":[["$","article",null,{"className":"lg:col-span-2","children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xl text-stone-600 leading-relaxed mb-6 font-medium pl-4 border-l-4 border-orange-500","children":"Working remotely should make you more productive. For most people it does the opposite. These are the habits, environment changes, and practices that research and experience show actually work."}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xs text-stone-500 dark:text-stone-400 mb-8 p-3 rounded-lg bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-900 border border-stone-200 dark:border-stone-800","children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Affiliate disclosure:"}]," This post may contain affiliate links to products we recommend. We earn a small commission when you buy through them, at no extra cost to you. As an Amazon Associate we earn from qualifying purchases. See our ",["$","$L7",null,{"href":"/disclaimer","className":"text-orange-600 dark:text-orange-400 hover:underline","children":"disclaimer"}]," for details."]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"prose","children":[["$","h2","0",{"id":"the-real-challenge-of-remote-work","children":"The Real Challenge of Remote Work"}],"$L29","$L2a","$L2b","$L2c","$L2d","$L2e","$L2f","$L30","$L31","$L32","$L33","$L34","$L35","$L36","$L37","$L38","$L39","$L3a","$L3b","$L3c","$L3d","$L3e","$L3f","$L40","$L41","$L42","$L43","$L44","$L45","$L46","$L47","$L48","$L49","$L4a","$L4b","$L4c","$L4d","$L4e","$L4f","$L50","$L51","$L52","$L53","$L54","$L55","$L56","$L57"]}],"$L58","$L59","$L5a"]}],"$L5b"]}]]}]
5c:I[94097,["/_next/static/chunks/0wjbooevnfpw4.js","/_next/static/chunks/0wu484j-81s3l.js"],"default"]
5d:I[24170,["/_next/static/chunks/0wjbooevnfpw4.js","/_next/static/chunks/0wu484j-81s3l.js"],"default"]
5e:I[55132,["/_next/static/chunks/0wjbooevnfpw4.js","/_next/static/chunks/0wu484j-81s3l.js"],"default"]
29:["$","p","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Working from home should be more productive than working in an office. You eliminate the commute, the open-plan noise, and the interruptions from passing colleagues. In theory, you are left with pure focused time."}}]
2a:["$","p","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"In practice, most people find the opposite. The structures that an office provides — a specific place for work, a defined start and end time, social accountability — do not exist at home unless you deliberately build them. This guide focuses on the practices that research and experience show genuinely work."}}]
2b:["$","h2","3",{"id":"environment","children":"Environment"}]
2c:["$","h3","4",{"id":"1-create-a-dedicated-work-surface","children":"1. Create a Dedicated Work Surface"}]
2d:["$","p","5",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The minimum viable home office is a dedicated surface used only for work. If your desk is in your bedroom, work follows you into sleep. If your dining table doubles as your office, the boundaries between work and life dissolve. One surface, used only for work, does more for focus than any technique."}}]
2e:["$","h3","6",{"id":"2-invest-in-your-chair-before-anything-else","children":"2. Invest in Your Chair Before Anything Else"}]
2f:["$","p","7",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Chronic discomfort is a sustained focus drain. A good ergonomic chair removes low-level physical distraction for hours at a time. This is the highest-return investment in your physical workspace — see our <a href=\"/blog/best-ergonomic-office-chairs-2026\" class=\"text-orange-600 dark:text-orange-400 hover:underline font-medium\">best ergonomic chairs guide</a> for the top options."}}]
30:["$","h3","8",{"id":"3-optimise-your-lighting","children":"3. Optimise Your Lighting"}]
31:["$","p","9",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Poor lighting causes fatigue you will misattribute to the work itself. Natural light perpendicular to your screen (not behind it, not in front of it) is ideal. Add a good desk lamp with adjustable colour temperature for evening work."}}]
32:["$","h3","10",{"id":"4-remove-your-phone-from-your-workspace","children":"4. Remove Your Phone from Your Workspace"}]
33:["$","p","11",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Research from the University of Texas found that the mere presence of a smartphone on a desk — even face-down — reduces available working memory. Put it in another room during deep work blocks. If you need it for 2-factor authentication, that is a 10-second visit, not a desk resident."}}]
34:["$","h2","12",{"id":"schedule-and-structure","children":"Schedule and Structure"}]
35:["$","h3","13",{"id":"5-choose-fixed-working-hours-and-communicate-them","children":"5. Choose Fixed Working Hours and Communicate Them"}]
36:["$","p","14",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Without fixed hours, work expands to fill all available time. Choose a start time, an end time, and communicate both to the people you work with. The hours matter less than the consistency."}}]
37:["$","h3","15",{"id":"6-start-work-with-a-written-plan","children":"6. Start Work With a Written Plan"}]
38:["$","p","16",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Before opening email or Slack, write three things: the most important task today, the second most important task, and the third. These three things define success for the day. Everything else is secondary."}}]
39:["$","h3","17",{"id":"7-use-time-blocking-not-just-a-to-do-list","children":"7. Use Time Blocking, Not Just a To-Do List"}]
3a:["$","p","18",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"A to-do list has no scarcity constraint — you can add items indefinitely. Time blocking assigns specific tasks to specific calendar slots, creating a commitment rather than a suggestion. Block at least two 90-minute deep work slots per day and protect them as you would a meeting. Our <a href=\"/blog/time-blocking-guide\" class=\"text-orange-600 dark:text-orange-400 hover:underline font-medium\">complete time blocking guide</a> covers the full system in depth."}}]
3b:["$","h3","19",{"id":"8-schedule-communication-windows","children":"8. Schedule Communication Windows"}]
3c:["$","p","20",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Check email and Slack at set times — 9 AM, 12 PM, 4 PM, for example — rather than responding as messages arrive. Outside those windows, close the applications. This is the single change that most reliably protects deep work time."}}]
3d:["$","h3","21",{"id":"9-implement-a-shutdown-ritual","children":"9. Implement a Shutdown Ritual"}]
3e:["$","p","22",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"A consistent end-of-day sequence explicitly closes the workday. A simple version: review today's tasks, write tomorrow's three priorities, close all work applications. Cal Newport, who popularised this concept, found that a verbal or written declaration (\"Shutdown complete\") helps the brain disengage, reducing the background processing that causes work to leak into evenings."}}]
3f:["$","h2","23",{"id":"focus-and-deep-work","children":"Focus and Deep Work"}]
40:["$","h3","24",{"id":"10-work-in-90-minute-blocks","children":"10. Work in 90-Minute Blocks"}]
41:["$","p","25",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Human ultradian rhythms — natural cycles of alertness — operate on roughly 90-minute intervals. Working in alignment with these cycles (90 minutes on, 15–20 minutes genuinely off) produces more total output than working in unbroken multi-hour stretches."}}]
42:["$","h3","26",{"id":"11-take-real-breaks","children":"11. Take Real Breaks"}]
43:["$","p","27",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"A genuine break means away from the screen and involving some movement. A 15-minute walk is near-optimal. Scrolling social media is not a break — it is a different kind of stimulation that does not restore the attention networks needed for focused work."}}]
44:["$","h3","28",{"id":"12-use-noise-management-actively","children":"12. Use Noise Management Actively"}]
45:["$","p","29",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"If your home environment is noisy, noise-cancelling headphones are not a luxury — they are a productivity tool. For cognitively demanding work, research suggests that ambient sound at moderate volume (coffee shop level, ~65 dB) is better than silence or music with lyrics."}}]
46:["$","h3","30",{"id":"13-do-the-hardest-task-first","children":"13. Do the Hardest Task First"}]
47:["$","p","31",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Your cognitive performance is highest in the first two hours after your brain is fully awake. Schedule your most demanding work — the task you are most likely to procrastinate on — in this window. Clearing it early creates momentum that carries through the rest of the day."}}]
48:["$","h3","32",{"id":"14-lower-the-activation-energy-to-start","children":"14. Lower the Activation Energy to Start"}]
49:["$","p","33",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The biggest obstacle to deep work is beginning. Make it easier: keep your most important project open in a tab, ready to resume. Keep your notes visible. Reduce the number of steps between sitting down and doing the work."}}]
4a:["$","h2","34",{"id":"separation-and-wellbeing","children":"Separation and Wellbeing"}]
4b:["$","h3","35",{"id":"15-create-a-commute-substitute","children":"15. Create a Commute Substitute"}]
4c:["$","p","36",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The commute served a function beyond transportation — it provided transition time between home and work modes. Without it, many remote workers start work before they are mentally present and finish before they have mentally left. A deliberate 15-minute walk before starting and another at the end of the day replicates this transition."}}]
4d:["$","h3","37",{"id":"16-establish-and-communicate-boundaries","children":"16. Establish and Communicate Boundaries"}]
4e:["$","p","38",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"If other people share your home during working hours, boundaries need to be explicit and communicated — not assumed. A closed door, headphones, or a visual indicator signal that you are not available. This requires a conversation, not just an expectation."}}]
4f:["$","h3","39",{"id":"17-leave-the-house-at-least-once-per-day","children":"17. Leave the House at Least Once Per Day"}]
50:["$","p","40",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Remote workers who do not leave the house regularly report higher rates of burnout, reduced creativity, and difficulty separating work from personal time. A single deliberate exit — a walk, an errand, lunch outside — significantly resets the perception of confinement that an all-day-at-home schedule can create."}}]
51:["$","h2","41",{"id":"output-and-reflection","children":"Output and Reflection"}]
52:["$","h3","42",{"id":"18-measure-outcomes-not-hours","children":"18. Measure Outcomes, Not Hours"}]
53:["$","p","43",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The most common remote work trap is optimising for the appearance of working — keeping Slack status green, staying at the desk, attending every optional meeting — rather than for actual output. Define what a productive day looks like in terms of results, not time spent."}}]
54:["$","h3","44",{"id":"19-review-weekly-adjust-monthly","children":"19. Review Weekly, Adjust Monthly"}]
55:["$","p","45",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Every Friday, spend 20 minutes reviewing the week: what moved forward, what stalled, and what system failure caused the stall. Adjust one thing for next week. Over a year, this habit compounds into a significantly better working practice."}}]
56:["$","h3","46",{"id":"20-prepare-tomorrow-before-you-finish-today","children":"20. Prepare Tomorrow Before You Finish Today"}]
57:["$","p","47",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The most reliable way to start the next day with momentum is to spend ten minutes at the end of today deciding what tomorrow looks like: the three tasks, and specifically the first one you will start with. When you sit down tomorrow, there is no decision to make — you begin immediately."}}]
58:["$","div",null,{"className":"mt-10 pt-8 border-t border-stone-200 dark:border-stone-800 flex flex-wrap items-center gap-3","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"text-sm font-medium text-stone-500 dark:text-stone-400","children":"Topic:"}],["$","$L7",null,{"href":"/blog?category=Productivity","className":"text-sm font-semibold px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-green-50 text-green-700 hover:opacity-80 transition-opacity","children":"Productivity"}],[["$","$L7","remote-work",{"href":"/blog/tag/remote-work","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 text-stone-600 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-orange-50 dark:hover:bg-orange-900/30 hover:text-orange-600 dark:hover:text-orange-400 transition-colors","children":["#","remote-work"]}],["$","$L7","productivity",{"href":"/blog/tag/productivity","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 text-stone-600 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-orange-50 dark:hover:bg-orange-900/30 hover:text-orange-600 dark:hover:text-orange-400 transition-colors","children":["#","productivity"]}],["$","$L7","habits",{"href":"/blog/tag/habits","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 text-stone-600 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-orange-50 dark:hover:bg-orange-900/30 hover:text-orange-600 dark:hover:text-orange-400 transition-colors","children":["#","habits"]}],["$","$L7","focus",{"href":"/blog/tag/focus","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 text-stone-600 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-orange-50 dark:hover:bg-orange-900/30 hover:text-orange-600 dark:hover:text-orange-400 transition-colors","children":["#","focus"]}],["$","$L7","work-from-home",{"href":"/blog/tag/work-from-home","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 text-stone-600 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-orange-50 dark:hover:bg-orange-900/30 hover:text-orange-600 dark:hover:text-orange-400 transition-colors","children":["#","work-from-home"]}]]]}]
59:["$","$L5c",null,{"title":"Work From Home Productivity: 20 Tips That Actually Make a Difference","slug":"work-from-home-productivity-tips"}]
5a:["$","div",null,{"className":"print:hidden","children":["$","$L5d",null,{}]}]

61:["$","$L7","remote-work-burnout-prevention",{"href":"/blog/remote-work-burnout-prevention","className":"flex gap-3 group","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"shrink-0 w-12 h-12 rounded-xl overflow-hidden","children":["$","img",null,{"src":"https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1545987796-200677ee1011?w=1200&h=800&fit=crop&auto=format&q=80","alt":"Remote Work Burnout: How to Recognise It and What Actually Prevents It","className":"w-full h-full object-cover group-hover:scale-110 transition-transform duration-300"}]}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-sm font-semibold text-stone-700 dark:text-stone-200 group-hover:text-orange-600 transition-colors line-clamp-2 leading-snug","children":"Remote Work Burnout: How to Recognise It and What Actually Prevents It"}]]}]
62:["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-stone-950 rounded-2xl p-6 text-center","children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"font-bold text-white text-lg mb-2","style":{"fontFamily":"'Playfair Display', Georgia, serif"},"children":"Enjoy this article?"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xs text-stone-400 mb-4","children":"Get more workspace ideas in your inbox."}],["$","$L7",null,{"href":"/contact","className":"inline-flex items-center gap-1.5 text-sm font-semibold px-4 py-2.5 rounded-xl text-white bg-orange-500 hover:bg-orange-600 transition-colors","children":["Get in touch ",["$","$L6",null,{"ref":"$undefined","iconNode":"$a:props:children:0:props:children:1:0:props:children:1:props:children:0:props:children:props:children:1:props:iconNode","className":"lucide-arrow-up-right","size":14}]]}]]}]
23:[["$","meta","0",{"charSet":"utf-8"}],["$","meta","1",{"name":"viewport","content":"width=device-width, initial-scale=1"}]]
63:I[27201,["/_next/static/chunks/01xlw8hd842-c.js","/_next/static/chunks/0d3shmwh5_nmn.js"],"IconMark"]
20:null
25:[["$","title","0",{"children":"Work From Home Productivity: 20 Tips That Actually Make a Difference"}],["$","meta","1",{"name":"description","content":"Working remotely should make you more productive. For most people it does the opposite. These are the habits, environment changes, and practices that research and experience show actually work."}],["$","link","2",{"rel":"manifest","href":"/manifest.webmanifest","crossOrigin":"$undefined"}],["$","link","3",{"rel":"canonical","href":"https://deskbly.com/blog/work-from-home-productivity-tips"}],["$","meta","4",{"property":"og:title","content":"Work From Home Productivity: 20 Tips That Actually Make a Difference"}],["$","meta","5",{"property":"og:description","content":"Working remotely should make you more productive. For most people it does the opposite. These are the habits, environment changes, and practices that research and experience show actually work."}],["$","meta","6",{"property":"og:url","content":"https://deskbly.com/blog/work-from-home-productivity-tips"}],["$","meta","7",{"property":"og:image","content":"https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1522202176988-66273c2fd55f?w=1200&h=800&fit=crop&auto=format&q=80"}],["$","meta","8",{"property":"og:image:width","content":"1200"}],["$","meta","9",{"property":"og:image:height","content":"800"}],["$","meta","10",{"property":"og:image:alt","content":"Work From Home Productivity: 20 Tips That Actually Make a Difference"}],["$","meta","11",{"property":"og:type","content":"article"}],["$","meta","12",{"property":"article:published_time","content":"2026-03-21"}],["$","meta","13",{"property":"article:modified_time","content":"2026-03-21"}],["$","meta","14",{"property":"article:author","content":"Sara Osei"}],["$","meta","15",{"property":"article:section","content":"Productivity"}],["$","meta","16",{"property":"article:tag","content":"remote-work"}],["$","meta","17",{"property":"article:tag","content":"productivity"}],["$","meta","18",{"property":"article:tag","content":"habits"}],["$","meta","19",{"property":"article:tag","content":"focus"}],["$","meta","20",{"property":"article:tag","content":"work-from-home"}],["$","meta","21",{"name":"twitter:card","content":"summary_large_image"}],["$","meta","22",{"name":"twitter:title","content":"Work From Home Productivity: 20 Tips That Actually Make a Difference"}],["$","meta","23",{"name":"twitter:description","content":"Working remotely should make you more productive. For most people it does the opposite. These are the habits, environment changes, and practices that research and experience show actually work."}],["$","meta","24",{"name":"twitter:image","content":"https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1522202176988-66273c2fd55f?w=1200&h=800&fit=crop&auto=format&q=80"}],["$","link","25",{"rel":"icon","href":"/icon?c0b95df96f490973","alt":"$undefined","type":"image/png","sizes":"32x32"}],["$","link","26",{"rel":"apple-touch-icon","href":"/apple-icon?f7f63e177608d30c","alt":"$undefined","type":"image/png","sizes":"180x180"}],["$","$L63","27",{}]]
